As much as I love Mark Wahlberg and his acting, I'mma have to disagree with Sony's casting decision to have him play Sully. I personally would have preferred J. K. Simmons to take on the iconic role coz of his mustache and his ability to portray gruff, commanding yet collected characters, like Sully, to a tee. Plus he's J.K. Simmons. I mean who wouldn't love to see J.K. Simmons. It is a bit late to be complaining about this though seeing as the movie has been all but completed already. No use crying over spilled milk. But if it doesn't flop like most game-to-movie adaptations and Sony considers a sequel, they should have Simmons play as Sully.

This movie looks fantastic, but it feels like they mixed up certain parts of the original story. Wow… I don’t like what they did to Sully in this. He comes off as a bit of an asshole. Hopefully he’s better in the full movie.

As for the ship… that’s been sitting there for 500 years. I’m not confident that the wood could sustain being hoisted-up by a helicopter, but this movie also has Nathan defying physics as he jumps from pallet to pallet behind a giant aircraft, so it’s definitely a “turn your brain off” type of film. In that respect, it’s very similar to the games I suppose.

I wish they hadn’t shown that mustache scene though. It seems to be the final shot of the film, so Sony likely only put it in there to shut up the community complaining about Sully not having his trademark ‘stache. It was clearly meant to be a nice reveal either at the end of the film, or after some time-jump deep into it, and now it’s ruined, and possibly the ending as well…

Look yall. We probably should just let the movie be the movie and the games be the games. This looks like a fun movie, but if we're expecting it to be exactly like the games, we might as well just go play them. I doubt the movie will be bad, but if we're going in there thinking there won't be a lot of liberties in the story then we won't enjoy the movie. It seems as though it was written to capture the essence of what Uncharted's story was and cram it into 1 movie with potential for a sequel -- that's it. I could be very wrong, but perhaps we shouldn't go in thinking that we're gonna get exactly what we want (which will differ between us anyway). I know no video game adaption that isn't just that -- an adaption. Even a show like Arcane has SOME people upset, and mostly because they came in expecting more than was realistic for them to get. I guess there's more to say in that, but I'm tired, which is ironically the reason I most likely even took the time to say any of this. Blah.

An good uncharted movie should be so damn easy to make, all you need is the right Sully and Nate and the two actors you choose need to have great chemistry, besides that you need great set pieces, fun dialogue, a cool villain and good action throughout. This movie seems to have a lot of these things. 

Unfortunately it’s missing the absolute most important thing if you ask me, Tom Holland doesn’t work as Drake, I love the idea of a younger Drake and Sully (I hate how they are taking you through an origin story, we didn’t need that because we already have Uncharted 3) getting to see their relationship as Drake grows into the great treasure hunter he becomes should be fun for sure. I just can’t get behind Holland as Drake, he doesn’t have the voice, he doesn’t have to sound like Nolan at all of course but he just doesn’t have that Drake confidence. Drake speaks with full confidence at all times while Holland sounds like he does in Spider-Man which is an unsure of himself teenage boy type tone, it doesn’t work for Drake at all, him and Peter Parker are very different characters. Wahlberg as Sully I can get behind, he probably can’t bring the same charm that Richard does but I think he will do well. You already had Wahlberg so you didn’t need a big name like Holland and I think it will hurt, at least when it comes to fans of the games. 

Another smaller thing is I don’t like them bringing in Sam, Uncharted 4 was a step back for the series and that Sam character didn’t work at all, he didn’t need to be in this. I think this should be an enjoyable movie but I don’t know if it will become a movie franchise,  all you need is for the audience to fall in love with Drake and Sully, if you can do that then you will keep fans coming back for more, with Holland I don’t see anywhere near the same charm. I honestly would have liked to see Dylan O Brien as Drake.

There also seem to be some timeline errors as well. Nate meets Sully in 1990 (as seen in uncharted 3 in Colombia, Nate was only 15 mind you) this makes it seem like they met when Nate was in his 20's, which must mean it's after 1998 (when Sam gets "killed" in the prison at the beginning of a thiefs end before going missing for 15 years). Lastly, we know Chloe is in the movie, drake meets Chloe between 01-06, so if sully knows where Sam is that means it has to be before 98. But since Chloe is in it, it must be after 01. On the same page with Chloe she didn't know sully before but in the trailer says "sully doesn't have friends I would know" the IMBD description says "The story is a prequel to the games" which can't be possible for timeline sake as I've been over but also let's be real how many times does one fall out a plane only being caught by the military supplies that happened to catch. There's just no possibility this is a prequel

Lowkey, they taking stunts and set pieces from Jackie Chan. He already did the banister sliding action and hanging/sliding from lights hundreds of feet off the ground. Police Story One/And CZ12 and Miracles have those exact stunts, lol. Looks like a cool movie, though Holland doesn't fit the role compared to how the game character looks. Plus, some of the scenes look rather Indiana Jones-cliche. Why's there always some party they crash where one person breaches security to steal the artifact while the other creates a distraction. Another Jones cliche is where the protagonists find the treasure first, then the bad guys walk in on them with guns in their hands or they force the protagonists to find it for them once they get to the final destination of where it could possibly be. Then everything crumbles once it's found, the bad guys perish, and the good guys get the treasure.

What they should do to make a good game adaptation is to create new storys in the uncharted universe respecting the characters (and the universe) and using things that make the games great, like those moments on the plane or on the train in the games, while also introducing new things like good characters, great places and more breathtaking moments. Not saying this movie will be good or bad, but recently Arcane showed what a good game adaptation should be, but people don't really seem get the reason why it was so great...

Why are they trying to overwhelm us with stuff from games?... instead of trying to make original story, that would possibly connect with games. Tom Holland is propably only one that makes sense to me in the cast, he was young Nate in uncharted 3 and to the story young Nate makes sense (don`t get confused i still do not like this story, but Tom is what makes sense in casting) Mark is so weird as Sully. what do we know Sully as? we know him as jokester(and mustache)... so... is "little young for bartender?" best you fucking got? Mark is known for being same character in every movie he makes... other "actors" like this are Dwayne Johnson and Ryan Reynolds... they act the same way in every movie, because they don`t act. they should have taken someone who really can adapt to an character, instead of someone who makes the character to adapt him. i see more cons with this than pros. i hope this is good movie, but i highly doubt it. for years video game movies have sucked... and it is not probably going to change for awhile

Look, I know everyone isn't fully onboard with this movie, but I'm seriously really looking forward it. I know that it isn't like shot-by-shot like the games, but the tone, the setting, and even the puzzle solving is very Uncharted to me, and if you ask me, I think this is an improvement over the last trailer. Adding the fact that they're going to explain the role of Sam in the story which was never adressed in the previous games. Overall, I'm not expecting some Citizen Kane movie out of this, just want a fun and entertaining adventure, that's all.

Edit: Guys, please calm down, I'm only mentioning it from my simple point of view. If the movie is already not for you, that's fine
